assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only: report.md, review-30001.md through review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md, and subtask-30002.md. All files are verification outputs (report, review classifications, sub-task descriptions). No source code files (e.g., .rs, .py, .js) are present in the outputs. The report describes analyzing the diff and verifying acceptance criteria but does not indicate any code modifications were made.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have individual classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md Review Comments Summary table lists all four comments with their classifications. The Findings section references comments 30001, 30002, 30003, and 30004. All comments were processed and classified before the report was generated.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merge, or any merge action. The report's overall status is 'WARN' and it concludes by noting 'reviewer-a has requested changes' and 'Two sub-tasks have been created for these code change requests.' The Review State is listed as 'CHANGES_REQUESTED' which is incompatible with merge. No merge commands or merge status appear anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 24: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | `tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` is a new file; no existing test files were modified |'. Additionally, the dedicated '## Test Change Classification' section (lines 66-69) states: '**Classification**: ADDITIVE' and 'All test changes are in a **new test file** (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`). No existing test files were modified.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a detailed '### Convention Upgrade Analysis' section (lines 19-31) that evaluates whether the suggestion matches conventions. It covers four points: '1. Database performance convention' (adding indexes on frequent WHERE clause columns), '2. Specificity of the request' (complete SQL statement provided), '3. Production performance impact' (list query latency), and '4. Migration completeness convention' (column and supporting index in same migration). The conclusion states: 'Given the alignment with standard database performance conventions... this is upgraded from suggestion to code change request.' report.md line 134 also references: 'Convention upgrade analysis confirms this aligns with database performance conventions.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path — whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies it as 'Code Change Request (upgraded from suggestion)' with 'Action: Create sub-task (subtask-30002.md)'. subtask-30002.md exists with a complete sub-task description. report.md Review Comments Summary confirms: '30002 | ... | Code change request (upgraded from suggestion) -- add partial index | Yes (subtask-30002.md)'. The sub-task was created via the convention upgrade path.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 25: '| Eval Quality | N/A | No eval result reviews exist (no reviews from github-actions[bot] with ## Eval Results and sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer) |'. This explicitly mentions all three detection criteria (github-actions[bot], ## Eval Results, sdlc-workflow/run-evals) and confirms N/A status. The Test Quality row (line 23) shows 'PASS' independently without being affected by Eval Quality.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created — the sub-tasks created are exclusively review feedback sub-tasks (labels ["ai-generated-jira", "review-feedback"]), not eval failure sub-tasks (labels ["ai-generated-jira", "eval-failure"]), because Eval Quality is N/A

Only two sub-tasks exist (subtask-30001.md and subtask-30002.md), both created for review feedback comments. Neither sub-task file contains any reference to eval-failure labels. The Eval Quality is N/A (line 25), so no eval failure sub-tasks were warranted or created.
